metadata.title: HUF-QIT-CoDa Knowledge Base
metadata.author: Peter Higgins — Rogue Wave Audio
metadata.purpose: Complete knowledge transfer for AI assistant continuity. Covers CoDaWork 2026 presentation focus, full HUF framework, quantum correspondence, and all empirical results.
metadata.usage: Load this JSON as context when working on any HUF, CoDa, quantum-CoDa, or energy-mix research.

conference.name: 11th International Workshop on Compositional Data Analysis (CoDaWork 2026)
conference.scientific_committee_chair: Juan José Egozcue (Technical University of Catalonia)

accepted_abstract.title: Compositional monitoring of energy-mix drift on the simplex
accepted_abstract.central_claim: No existing monitoring framework performs compositional change detection at the carrier level with formal perturbation-based drift measurement.
four_defeat_conditions: A published system using the Aitchison metric on energy-mix compositions to detect drift
A published system measuring change as perturbation (compositional ratio) rather than arithmetic difference
A published system at individual carrier level within the simplex (not aggregated to fossil/clean)
A theoretical proof that CoDa adds no information beyond percentage-based monitoring

perturbation_difference.description: Compositional ratio between consecutive periods, closed to the simplex. The Aitchison-group analogue of subtraction.

aitchison_distance.description: Natural metric on the simplex. Invariant under perturbation, permutation, and subcomposition. Equals the Aitchison norm of the perturbation difference.

effective_diversity.description: Effective number of carriers. Equals D when all carriers contribute equally, approaches 1 when one dominates.

drift_flag.description: Self-calibrated from each country's own distribution of year-on-year distances. No external threshold needed.

drift_flags.interpretation: Final nuclear shutdown (April 2023), full-year effect. Nuclear 1.4% → 0.0%

Japan.note: Fukushima disaster March 2011; compositional shock fully absorbed by 2013-2014 annual data

Ascari_Fiori_et_al.title: Compositional k-prototypes: CoDa clustering with zeros
Ascari_Fiori_et_al.relevance: Energy mix clustering with essential zeros. Complementary — they solve zeros, we monitor drift.

Vega_Baquero_Santolino.title: Proportionality between allocations in asset management
Vega_Baquero_Santolino.relevance: Portfolio allocation as composition using Aitchison geometry. Independently confirms the Wall Street CoDa-finance insight.

Narayana_Chotirmall.title: The cost of ignoring compositionality: CoDa unmasks treatment effects in COPD clinical trials
Narayana_Chotirmall.relevance: Closest methodological neighbour. Uses perturbation and Aitchison norm on longitudinal microbiome data. Clinical trial, not monitoring protocol.

open_question.question: What is the formal relationship between effective diversity N_eff = exp(H) and the Aitchison norm ‖x‖_A = ‖clr(x)‖?
open_question.observation: Both quantify how 'spread' a composition is. They are correlated but not identical. A clean inequality relating them likely involves dimension D.
open_question.status: Posed to the CoDa community at CoDaWork 2026

eitt.name: Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer
eitt.core_principle: Valid decimation of compositional time series must preserve Shannon entropy

geometric_mean_closed.description: Aitchison barycenter (Fréchet mean under Aitchison metric). Equivalent to averaging in CLR space and back-transforming.

arithmetic_mean.description: Standard average. Not a valid CoDa operation — does not respect simplex geometry.

why_it_works: clr(x̄_G) = (1/k)Σclr(x(t)) — CLR coordinates average linearly under geometric mean
Shannon entropy is concave on the simplex; Jensen's inequality applies differently to geometric vs arithmetic means
Near-preservation holds because compositions within a window are locally similar (small perturbation steps)
eitt.quantum_analogue: Unitarity — time evolution in quantum mechanics preserves |ψ|² = 1 and von Neumann entropy. EITT is the compositional analogue.
eitt.status: Empirical finding. Not yet formally proved. Seeded at CoDaWork 2026 Slide 15.

huf_framework.name: Higgins Unity Framework

chi_bar.definition: Geometric mean of proportional shares within a compositional group

cv_chi.definition: Coefficient of variation of the compositional parts

W.name: Winding ratio
W.definition: Ratio of path length to displacement in compositional space

governance_efficiency.definition: Derived from W — measures how directly a system moves toward its stated goal

k1_distance.definition: Distance metric in the HUF framework

huf_framework.doctrine: Open-loop: HUF-GOV (observation) not HUF-CLS (control). Instrument reads, human decides, loop stays open.

coda_foundations.name: Compositional Data Analysis
key_references: Aitchison, J. (1986). The Statistical Analysis of Compositional Data. Chapman & Hall.
Pawlowsky-Glahn, V., Egozcue, J.J. & Tolosana-Delgado, R. (2015). Modeling and Analysis of Compositional Data. Wiley.
Egozcue, J.J., Pawlowsky-Glahn, V., Mateu-Figueras, G. & Barceló-Vidal, C. (2003). Isometric logratio transformations. Mathematical Geology, 35(3), 279-300.
Filzmoser, P., Hron, K. & Templ, M. (2018). Applied Compositional Data Analysis. Springer.
Martín-Fernández, J.A., Barceló-Vidal, C. & Pawlowsky-Glahn, V. (2003). Dealing with zeros. Mathematical Geology, 35(3), 253-278.
Jost, L. (2006). Entropy and diversity. Oikos, 113(2), 363-375.

simplex.definition: S^D = { x ∈ ℝ^D : xᵢ > 0, Σxᵢ = κ }

log_ratio_transforms.ALR: alr(x) = ( ln(x₁/xD), …, ln(x_{D-1}/xD) ) — additive log-ratio, asymmetric
log_ratio_transforms.CLR: clr(x) = ( ln(xᵢ) − (1/D)Σln(xⱼ) ) — centred log-ratio, symmetric but singular
log_ratio_transforms.ILR: ilr(x) = Ψ·clr(x) — isometric log-ratio, orthonormal basis in S^D

coda_foundations.variation_matrix: T = [var(ln(xᵢ/xⱼ))]_{i,j} — the fundamental dispersion measure for compositions

quantum_correspondence.name: HUF-QIT (Quantum Information Theory correspondence)
quantum_correspondence.core_insight: The simplex constraint Σxᵢ = 1 is structurally identical to the Hilbert norm Σ|ψᵢ|² = 1. CoDa operations map formally to quantum operations.

nine_isomorphisms.explanation: Just as quantum states can be expressed in different bases, compositions can be expressed in different log-ratio coordinate systems.
Decoherence selects pointer states; domain knowledge selects meaningful balances.
Removing carriers and re-closing is the compositional analogue of tracing out a quantum subsystem.
Valid time evolution preserves total probability and entropy in both frameworks.
Adding a new carrier forces all other shares to adjust — like environmental decoherence.
You cannot extract a subcomposition and expect it to behave identically to the full composition — information is lost.
Different models observing the same system yield different compositions, like different observers in QM.
Perturbation alters a composition without breaking the simplex constraint, like a unitary operation.
The uniform composition is the maximum-entropy state — the compositional 'ground state'.

spooky_action_reinterpretation.claim: Spooky action at a distance is constraint propagation, not a mysterious force.
spooky_action_reinterpretation.mechanism: In QM: Σ|ψᵢ|² = 1, so measuring one component instantly constrains others. In CoDa: Σxᵢ = 1, so changing one carrier's share forces all others to adjust. Not hidden variables — a global topological property of the normalisation constraint.
spooky_action_reinterpretation.consistent_with: Bell's theorem. No hidden variables needed. The correlations arise from the constraint itself.

aes256_safety.question: Does the CoDa-quantum correspondence threaten encryption?
aes256_safety.answer: No. Simplex is polynomial-dimensional (D-1). Quantum computing power comes from exponential dimensionality (2^n qubits). Same correlation mathematics, different computational scale. The correspondence is mathematical, not computational.

bell_test.name: Compositional CHSH-analogue Bell test
bell_test.key_finding: Gas acts as crossover frequency / entangling gate — all top Bell violation configurations involve Gas bridging fossil and clean subcompositions.
bell_test.closure_boost: 1.00× for individual log-ratios (Aitchison's theorem — ln(xᵢ/xⱼ) cancels closure divisor). Entanglement appears in correlation COMBINATIONS, not individual log-ratios.

tools.name: Entanglement Entropy
tools.method: CLR → correlation matrix |R| → density matrix ρ → eigenvalues → von Neumann S(ρ) = −Σλᵢln(λᵢ)

tools.name: Leggett-Garg Inequality
tools.description: Temporal Bell test. Dichotomic observable Q = sign(ln(ΣFossil/ΣClean))
tools.scans: All triples of time points for K₃ > 1

tools.name: Schmidt Decomposition
tools.method: Cross-correlation matrix M (Fossil×Clean), SVD, Schmidt coefficients

tools.name: Compositional Fidelity
tools.method: F(ρ,σ) = (Tr√(√ρ σ √ρ))² on CLR density matrices
tools.description: Measures similarity between two compositional states

wall_street_application.insight: Portfolio allocation (Σwᵢ = 1) is a composition. All HUF-QTM tools apply directly to finance.
wall_street_application.portfolio_as_simplex: n assets with weights w₁, …, wₙ where Σwᵢ = 1 form a composition on S^n
wall_street_application.confirmed_by: Vega Baquero & Santolino (CoDaWork 2026, p.57) — Aitchison geometry on IBEX 35 portfolios

Book_0.title: HUF-QIT Primer
Book_0.content: Standalone foundations: CoDa geometry, HUF framework, quantum essentials, the bridge. 16 sections + 2 appendices, notation guide with 30+ symbols.

Book_1.title: The Observer Problem in Compositional Data Analysis
Book_1.content: Full IMRAD theoretical paper: nine isomorphisms, compositional Bell test, spooky action as constraint propagation, open-loop doctrine.

Book_2.title: HUF-QTM Toolkit and Empirical Results
Book_2.content: Full IMRAD empirical paper: all 5 quantum tools applied to 35 NGFS scenarios, financial compositions section, AES-256 boundary statement.

show: The monitoring protocol (perturbation, Aitchison distance, effective diversity)
EMBER data: Germany, Japan, UK, 9 carriers, 2000-2025
Three showcase results with drift flags
Falsifiable claim with four defeat conditions
EITT seed on Slide 15 (2 minutes, end of talk)
keep_in_back_pocket: Bell test results on NGFS data

deflection_phrases.quantum: That's a different paper for a different venue. Happy to discuss at the break.
deflection_phrases.eitt_detail: I have the empirical results — happy to walk through them over coffee.
deflection_phrases.finance: Vega Baquero here at CoDaWork is presenting on exactly that — portfolio allocation as composition.

time_series_landscape_codawork2026.summary: Nobody else at CoDaWork 2026 is presenting a compositional time-series monitoring protocol with perturbation-based drift detection.

closest_neighbours.what: Perturbation + Aitchison norm on longitudinal microbiome (COPD trial)
closest_neighbours.difference: Clinical before/after, not running monitoring protocol

closest_neighbours.what: Longitudinal microbiome with PCA + mixed effects
Trajectory clustering of weekly time-use compositions
closest_neighbours.difference: Clustering trajectories with Euclidean distance, not Aitchison

closest_neighbours.what: FANOVA of temperature densities over time (Vietnam)
closest_neighbours.difference: Density-valued functional data, not simplex compositions
